sections.markdown:
## Course Cues
- Official event: 21.1 km closed-road half marathon on Sunday 2026-06-21, starting at 7:00 am.
- Start on O'Connell Street, run through Parramatta Park, the CBD, Western Sydney University and the river foreshore, then finish inside CommBank Stadium.
- Official route links: Strava route 3469133686913116518 and Garmin Connect course 475899978.
- Runner guide lists elevation at about +/-140 m, with aid stations at 5 km on George Street, 12 km on MacArthur Street, and 15 km in Parramatta Park.
- Map read: early CBD turns and loops before 5 km, a longer rhythm section out through the river/WSU side around the middle of the race, then Parramatta Park around 15-20 km before the stadium finish.
- Treat this as a rhythm-and-control course, not a perfectly flat time-trial course. Use effort and grade-adjusted feel on rises, turns, and GPS-noisy city sections.

## Race Targets
- A goal: sub-1:45:00, about 4:59/km.
- Good-day stretch: 1:44:00-1:44:30 only if the effort is still controlled after 15 km.
- B goal: 1:45:00-1:46:30 with no blow-up and a composed finish.
- C goal: beat the HOKA half-marathon anchor by racing controlled and finishing hard. Current anchor: 21.098 km in 1:48:29, plus full moving result of 21.469 km in 1:50:24.
- VDOT context: 4:59/km is inside the current workout-supported range, but it is not a free pace. Earn it by staying calm early and attacking late.

## Pace Plan
- 0-3 km: 5:03-5:06/km. Find space, avoid weaving, and let the race come to you.
- 3-10 km: settle at 4:59-5:01/km. Checkpoint target: 5 km around 24:55-25:10 and 10 km around 49:50-50:10.
- 10-15 km: if breathing and legs are controlled, squeeze to 4:56-4:59/km. If not, hold 5:00-5:03/km and keep the race alive. Checkpoint target: 15 km around 1:14:40-1:15:05.
- 15-18 km: protect form through the park and any turn cost. Do not surge the rises; keep cadence and effort steady.
- 18 km-finish: if still strong, progress to 4:50-4:55/km. Checkpoint target: 20 km around 1:39:30-1:40:00, then push the final 1.1 km hard into the stadium.
- If GPS pace looks jumpy in the CBD, park, or stadium precinct, trust elapsed time at km markers over instant pace.

## Heart-Rate Guardrails
- 0-5 km: let HR rise naturally. Avoid sitting above 170-172 bpm unless it is a brief hill or GPS/reading spike.
- 5-15 km: expect high 160s to mid 170s if the effort is right. If HR is over 180 bpm before 12 km and breathing is not controlled, switch to the B plan.
- 15 km-finish: high 170s or low 180s is acceptable if the legs are still moving well and form is intact.

## Fuel, Hydration, and Logistics
- Bib collection: collect at the CommBank Stadium expo on Friday 19 June, 12:00 pm-7:00 pm, or Saturday 20 June, 9:00 am-6:00 pm. No race-morning bib collection.
- Travel: use public transport if possible; the runner guide says major road closures start from 3:00 am and runners should arrive at least 90 minutes before the wave start.
- Breakfast: normal race breakfast 2.5-3 hours before the start. No new foods.
- Warm-up: 10-12 minutes easy, a few drills, then 4 relaxed strides. Finish the strides 5-10 minutes before start time.
- Gels: take one gel 10-15 minutes before the start. If relying on course water, take gels just before the 5 km and 12 km aid stations. Use the 15 km station for water/electrolyte and only take another gel if it is already practiced and needed.
- Hydration: small sips at aid stations. Do not overdrink, and do not skip water with gels unless carrying your own fluid.

## Contingencies
- If the first 5 km is slower than 25:20 because of crowds or turns, do not sprint it back. Recover gradually from 6-15 km.
- If the first 5 km feels too hot, hold 5:03-5:07/km until 15 km, then reassess.
- If you feel excellent at 10 km, wait. The first real permission to press is 13-15 km, not 10 km.
- If Saturday basketball happens or the legs feel flat in the warm-up, ignore the 1:44 stretch until after 15 km.
- Final rule: the race starts emotionally at 15 km and physically at 18 km. Before that, stay boring.
